name: nyx
summary: Nyx (goddess of the night in Greek mythology) is a self-contained script for cleaning forensic traces on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `chmod +x nyx.sh`; `sudo ./nyx.sh --dry-run`. Nyx (goddess of the night in Greek mythology) is a self-contained script for cleaning forensic traces on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: Nyx (goddess of the night in Greek mythology) is a self-contained script for cleaning forensic traces on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Nyx (goddess of the night in Greek mythology) is a self-contained script for cleaning forensic traces on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use nyx because Nyx (goddess of the night in Greek mythology) is a self-contained script for cleaning forensic traces on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for nyx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chmod +x nyx.sh` as documented upstream for nyx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo ./nyx.sh --dry-run` as documented upstream for nyx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo ./nyx.sh --force` as documented upstream for nyx.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair nyx with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `nyx --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
